diagnoses.name: Non-ST elevation (NSTEMI) myocardial infarction
diagnoses.evidence_span: The clinical presentation and biomarkers were consistent with a non-ST elevation myocardial infarction (NSTEMI)

diagnoses.name: Dissection of abdominal aorta
diagnoses.evidence_span: CT angiography confirmed Type B dissection of the abdominal aorta extending from the left subclavian artery

diagnoses.name: Coronary artery stenosis of left anterior descending artery
diagnoses.evidence_span: Coronary angiography with fluoroscopic imaging of multiple coronary arteries revealed a critical stenosis in the left anterior descending artery with significant thrombus burden

diagnoses.name: Acute myocardial injury
diagnoses.evidence_span: Troponin I was elevated at 2.8 ng/mL, consistent with acute myocardial injury

diagnoses.name: Gastro-esophageal reflux disease without esophagitis
diagnoses.evidence_span: the patient has a history of gastro-esophageal reflux disease without esophagitis

diagnoses.name: Dyspnea
diagnoses.evidence_span: presented to the emergency department with acute onset substernal chest pain radiating to the left arm, associated with dyspnea and diaphoresis

diagnoses.name: Hypertension
diagnoses.evidence_span: Vital signs on admission: BP 168/92 mmHg

procedures.name: Coronary angiography
procedures.evidence_span: Coronary angiography with fluoroscopic imaging of multiple coronary arteries revealed a critical stenosis in the left anterior descending artery

procedures.name: CT angiography
procedures.evidence_span: CT angiography confirmed Type B dissection of the abdominal aorta

procedures.name: Percutaneous coronary intervention with thrombectomy
procedures.evidence_span: The patient underwent emergent coronary intervention with extirpation of thrombotic material from the left anterior descending artery via percutaneous approach

procedures.name: EKG
procedures.evidence_span: Initial 12-lead EKG demonstrated ST segment depressions in the anterolateral leads

procedures.name: Influenza vaccination (quadrivalent)
procedures.evidence_span: As part of preventive care during this admission, the patient received the quadrivalent influenza vaccine

medications.name: Nitroglycerin
medications.evidence_span: Nitroglycerin 0.3 mg sublingual as needed for chest pain

medications.name: Pantoprazole
medications.evidence_span: Pantoprazole 40 mg PO daily (for reflux disease management)

medications.name: IV nitroglycerin infusion
medications.evidence_span: The patient received IV nitroglycerin infusion

medications.name: IV hydralazine
medications.evidence_span: IV hydralazine for blood pressure management
